title: tingulonga paniculata
intro: Tingulonga paniculata, also known as African boxwood or yellow fever tree, is a medicinal plant native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its antimalarial, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Tingulonga paniculata have been linked to various health benefits, including the reduction of fever, relief from pain and inflammation, and the treatment of malaria and other infectious diseases. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water or boiling the roots in water to create teas or decoctions that can be used to treat various health conditions.
